stem: In the xy-plane, a parabola has vertex (9, −14) and intersects the x-axis at two points. If the equation of the parabola is written in the form y = ax² + bx + c, where a, b, and c are constants, which of the following could be the value of a + b + c?
explanation: In vertex form y = a(x − 9)² − 14, we have a + b + c = f(1) = a(1 − 9)² − 14 = 64a − 14. Since the parabola opens upward (it crosses the x-axis with vertex below it), a > 0, so 64a − 14 > −14. Only −12 exceeds −14 (with a = 1/32).
